title: Failed to GetDriver graph fuse-overlayfs
body:
### Issue Description

podman fails to use fuse-overlayfs

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. set fuse-overlayfs in config
2. run podman


### Describe the results you received

```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver="fuse-overlayfs"

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
$ bin/podman info
ERRO[0000] Failed to GetDriver graph fuse-overlayfs /home/leo/.local/share/containers/storage 
Error: failed to GetDriver graph fuse-overlayfs /home/leo/.local/share/containers/storage: driver not supported
$ lsmod | grep fuse
fuse                  176128  5
```

### Describe the results you expected

podman should work

### podman info output

```yaml
$ bin/podman info 
ERRO[0000] Failed to GetDriver graph fuse-overlayfs /home/leo/.local/share/containers/storage 
Error: failed to GetDriver graph fuse-overlayfs /home/leo/.local/share/containers/storage: driver not supported
$ bin/podman --version
podman version 5.0.0-dev
$ uname -a
Linux debian 6.1.0-13-amd64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Debian 6.1.55-1 (2023-09-29) x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

--

### Additional information

A build script that usually took 10 minutes now takes two hours and for simple commands like a `sed` on a small text file it takes 10 minutes and runs out of 147GB or disk space using vfs. I saw that fuse-overlayfs was the recommended driver but fear it won't fix this.

comments.body: Driver should be "overlay", and it will use fuse-overlay.
